pi_items.source: Matches task description, which casts the agent as a Veterinarian ('Dr. Patel').
Matches task description to convert a residential apartment shell into a veterinary clinic.
Verified via setup_task.sh which programmatically generates the file with this exact name by stripping furniture from the official SweetHome3DExample.sh3d sample.
Verified via export_result.sh and verifier.py logic which explicitly track and score new walls, new doors, text labels, and categorized furniture counts.

privileged_info_summary: Verified the task operates on a synthetically generated 'veterinary_clinic_starter.sh3d' shell derived from a real Sweet Home 3D example file. The task accurately requires an agent acting as a Veterinarian in the Veterinary Services industry to perform furniture_placement, wall_creation, door_window_placement, and label_placement. Confirmed via script analysis that the total points sum to 100 and the passing threshold is precisely 70. However, the verifier exhibits a minor logic flaw: since it relies on a global count of item types rather than zone-specific counts, placing 6 total chairs will satisfy both the reception and examination room chair requirements concurrently without needing the 13 chairs explicitly outlined in the prompt.
